title: compliance — model relationships
description: Eight owned entities with a tight internal graph. External graph is intentionally minimal: the module publishes traits + attributes so downstream models opt in without adding foreign keys back to this module. DSAR artefacts point at storage::File; consent + legal-hold + safeguarding are polymorphic on subject (subject_type + subject_id).

internal.description: Restrict on delete so a category with active records cannot be removed. Enforced additionally at the ConsentCategoryObserver.deleting level with a specific error message.
Artefacts cascade with their parent DSAR. On soft-delete of Dsar, artefacts soft-delete too.
One RetentionRun references every LegalHold that was active during its execution + skipped rows because of it. Enables the audit query 'which rows did we NOT purge in run X + why?'

outbound.description: Nullable — platform-default rows carry tenant_id=NULL and are visible to every tenant. Tenant overrides carry a tenant_id and shadow the platform default.
Every consent record is tenant-scoped. On tenant erasure the cascade runs a compliance::CascadeConsentErasure listener that writes summary rows into audit before dropping.
Set only when the consent category `requires_guardian=true`. Non-null indicates parental / guardian consent for a minor.
Every DSAR is scoped to a tenant. Cross-tenant DSARs (rare) require the requester to file one DSAR per tenant they have data in.
Polymorphic subject. Typically a user::User but can be a TenantContact or an ExternalSubject (a walk-in inquiry from a non-account holder).
Platform-admin who is triaging / reviewing this DSAR. Null before triage.

Points at the actual export file (ZIP / JSON / CSV per artefact format). Null until assembly completes. On file erasure (retention purge of the DSAR export), stays as an audit-only row with file_id=null.
Every hold is scoped to at most one tenant. Platform-level holds (rare) use tenant_id=NULL + require super_admin authorisation.

outbound.to: subject (any HasLegalHold model or null for scope-level holds)
outbound.description: Subject-scoped hold. Null when the hold is tenant / case / class scoped.
Restrict on delete — the user who applied a hold cannot be removed while the hold is active (compliance audit-trail integrity).
Two-person approval requirement. Restrict same as applied_by.
One RetentionRun per (tenant, sweep). Platform-level sweeps (rare, for platform-owned entities) use tenant_id=NULL.
Platform-admin who last edited the subprocessor row. Historical trail preserved via HasAuditable snapshots.
The minor at the centre of the safeguarding concern.
The reporter. Nullable for anonymous reports (feature-flagged — default off; enterprise tenants may enable).
The tenant's safeguarding officer handling the case. Null until triage.

inbound.description: Downstream modules attach to compliance via TRAITS + ATTRIBUTES, not via foreign keys. Every consumer opts in through composition; compliance's registries discover them at boot.
consumer_patterns: any model with `use HasConsent` — the model becomes a polymorphic subject on ConsentRecord (subject_type = model class, subject_id = model primary key)
any model with `use IsRetentionAware` — the RetentionRunner enumerates its rows during the sweep + writes outcomes into RetentionRun
any model with `use HasLegalHold` — LegalHoldGate can freeze its rows
any model with `#[DsarExportable]` / `#[DsarErasable]` — becomes a DsarContributor
any model with `#[LegalHoldable]` — rows can be scoped by a LegalHold with scope=`class`
consumer_examples_planned: user::User — the primary subject. HasConsent + IsDsarContributor + #[DsarExportable] + #[DsarErasable] + #[LegalHoldable]
storage::File — IsRetentionAware + HasLegalHold + #[DsarExportable] (contributes file metadata to DSAR)
tenancy::TenantContact — HasConsent (for marketing consent on billing contacts)

consumed.usage: DsarArtefact.file_id — the assembled export ZIP / JSON is materialised as a storage::File with kind=`export_artifact`. The file's retention window (30 days per storage::retention.json) governs when the DSAR artefact link expires.
Every entity is tenant-scoped via BelongsToTenant (except platform-default ConsentCategory + platform-owned Subprocessor).
Every DSAR state transition, legal-hold apply / release, subprocessor change, consent grant / withdrawal, and safeguarding-incident touch writes an entry via HasAuditable.
DSAR + LegalHold + SafeguardingIncident state transitions surface in the tenant activity feed via HasActivityLog. ConsentRecord changes DO NOT (too noisy — lives in audit only).

compliance.dsars.month consumed per DSAR submission. compliance.legal-holds.max slot consumed per active hold. compliance.retention-runs.month consumed per manually-dispatched sweep (nightly automatic sweeps do not consume the quota).
12 notification categories seeded under the `compliance` namespace for DSAR lifecycle, safeguarding lifecycle, breach detection, subprocessor changes.

DsarDeliveredNotification uses a signed-URL body pointing at the export artefact (storage-issued). LegalHoldAppliedNotification / BreachDetectedNotification are email-only (never SMS / push — too sensitive for those channels by default).
